[2018-03-04 23:46] elyot: @gex you can shift-tab to switch to the half-open view in-game, this is documented only in the post-game tips though...
[2018-03-04 23:46] elyot: @Wiwiweb Plan is to open up play.prismata.net to everyone around the same time as we make Prismata fully F2P on steam
[2018-03-04 23:49] elyot: @RiFT (#RIPBrowserers) Every time the server does a "matching round", it goes through all the queues in a random order and does as many matches as it can in that specific queue, and the randomness is weighted toward picking faster time controls earlier first, basically bullet has more "raffle tickets" in the draw. The reason is that we want players to spend "an equal amount of time" playing each time control, not "an equal number of matches" at each time control
[2018-03-04 23:50] elyot: self.queueWeights = {"NoTime":2, "Slow":2, "Medium Slow":3, "Normal":4, "Quick":6, "Rapid":9, "Blitz":15, "Bullet":30}
[2018-03-04 23:51] elyot: so basically imagine you put that many coloured balls into an urn and pull them out one at a time, and match the queues in order of "when their colour was pulled first"
[2018-03-04 23:59] wiwiweb: Thanks Elyot, just to confirm, free players will have access to everything but the campaign, right?
[2018-03-05 00:00] wiwiweb: You've said a few times the campaign is where new players are expected to start, and where we show them the bases
[2018-03-05 00:02] elyot: chapter 1 will be for everyone

[2018-03-06 08:44] coscott: Why did you decide to make apollo snipe a gauss cannon on the same turn? It seems like a simpler interpretation of the rules to have damage assigned at the end of turn, with the defender deciding blocker order and attacking player deciding breached damage order, but no other actions
[2018-03-06 08:45] coscott: I guess the quality of life of being able to assign damage, then buy/click things is pretty nice
[2018-03-06 08:46] coscott: yeah, I take it back it is not obvious to me which is simpler, but I am curious why you went that way
[2018-03-06 08:47] elyot: felt disappointing not to be able to do it
[2018-03-06 08:49] coscott: actually, thinking about it more, I could see it going either way, if fragile blockers didnt exist, but given that you cant snipe fragile blockers, it seems weird that you can snipe gauss cannon

[2018-03-06 21:41] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): will the campaign answer the question how lucina gives birth to the perforators?
[2018-03-06 21:47] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Eats a Drone through her orifice, pops a Perforator back out.
[2018-03-06 21:55] elyot: @Deleted User not really, we just do it ourselves

[2018-03-06 22:21] elyot: @Spyrfyr it's intended. Does it look ugly? We can fix
[2018-03-06 22:23] elyot: @StrShPlyr We tried literally dozens of starting configurations including ones with custom units etc. and didn't find any that were significantly better than D^6 vs D^7
[2018-03-06 22:23] elyot: In fact we found a lot that were a lot worse
[2018-03-06 22:23] elyot: and people really like D^6 and D^7 because it's very elegant and simple and they get a little "insight moment" when they realize that it's the same thing as D^7 vs D^8

[2018-03-06 22:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Was just asking because it came up on overboardgames' stream. I think it'd look better if it was more obviously a "window" that opens up on top of main menu. Right now it doesn't look very natural
[2018-03-06 22:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Also idk how different resolutions affect this, if at all
[2018-03-06 22:24] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Players joke about it being a P1 resign.
[2018-03-06 22:25] elyot: @StrShPlyr Redeemer is pretty balanced according to our stats, but if it was a problem, we would change redeemer, not the starting configuration

[2018-03-06 22:27] Deleted User (Deleted User): 1 gold barely affects it in those cases and really punishes p2 in all the others

[2018-03-06 22:27] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Flowing the extra leverage into Tempo.
[2018-03-06 22:28] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): But ussually not very one-sided, balance.

The tier system already prevents players from getting punished for playing ranked, and blockading the ranked system decreases the number of new players on the ranked ladder and increases the odds of hitting a vet in the first few games.

[2018-03-07 22:19] elyot: @Redrame Level 20 is really the basic minimum you need to be familiar enough I think. It's not that much.

[2018-03-07 22:20] elyot: @Polari I'll take a look
[2018-03-07 22:21] elyot: @Polari I don't see any crashes on your account since feb 27
[2018-03-07 22:21] p0lari (Polari): hmm, maybe the server sees it as just me quitting
[2018-03-07 22:21] mqp: elyot i promise i don't want to get on your case about this but i'm genuinely curious -- do you have a lot of user playtesting evidence that most people need to do about a level-20-ish amount of casual games/puzzles/missions (how many hours of thinking is that, half a dozen?) before they are minimally competent, or is it just your common sense?
[2018-03-07 22:21] p0lari (Polari): the client froze and I forcibly closed it
[2018-03-07 22:23] Deleted User (Deleted User): How many hours is level 20 or so?
[2018-03-07 22:25] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): I made lvl 20 in like 1-2 hours
[2018-03-07 22:25] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): that´s when you can easily rush through all the combat training missions though
[2018-03-07 22:26] redrame: yeah, lv 20 for a new player is a long ass time
[2018-03-07 22:26] Deleted User (Deleted User): levels seem pointless if u can grind them that fast imo
[2018-03-07 22:26] redrame: long enough that people will drop a game if they arent interested
[2018-03-07 22:27] redrame: like I think the gate for casual play should be about the gate for ranked play
[2018-03-07 22:27] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): well do you really want people to get their ass handed over on ladder
[2018-03-07 22:28] redrame: you could even just inform players of the risk
[2018-03-07 22:28] elyot: tbh most of the dev team thinks it should be 40 but we have the ability to override it
[2018-03-07 22:28] redrame: like you do with everything else challenging in the game
[2018-03-07 22:28] elyot: right now it warns you if it's less than 40
[2018-03-07 22:28] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): most people like unlocking things, so having cool things to unlock is good
[2018-03-07 22:28] Deleted User (Deleted User): How much do new people dislike losing on ranked
[2018-03-07 22:28] redrame: the classic "appeal to an even more extreme opinion"
[2018-03-07 22:29] elyot: Like we have a lot of data that shows people who start playing ranked too early often quit
[2018-03-07 22:29] redrame: alright, that I can buy
[2018-03-07 22:29] redrame: I do think if people took the warning seriously they would just stop playing ranked...
[2018-03-07 22:29] redrame: but maybe I overrate people's intelligence
[2018-03-07 22:31] p0lari (Polari): is that data from around kickstarter when there were more people or during the time when ranked queue has been a horrible shark pool?
[2018-03-07 22:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): I can't argue with the facts, but it's super surprising to me that happens
[2018-03-07 22:31] p0lari (Polari): I'd think ideally noobs would hit ranked queue and merrily get matched vs other noobs
[2018-03-07 22:31] crash_overlord: I mean pretty much every game except HS has a "get to x level before you can play ranked" thing
[2018-03-07 22:32] mqp: come on crash you know that's not true for any reference class of games other than "literally the games you are thinking of"
[2018-03-07 22:32] crash_overlord: I'm pretty sure theres a valid reason for that implementation everywhere, even if its as simple as "people like unlocking things"
[2018-03-07 22:32] redrame: I mean HS also starts you in the bronzest of the bronze
[2018-03-07 22:32] redrame: personally I think that was a very successful strategy compared to gating off content
[2018-03-07 22:32] 1000mh (Henry): Isn't the casual queue the solution to this?
[2018-03-07 22:33] redrame: yeah, this is all a continuation to my question... people are asking about the data
[2018-03-07 22:42] elyot: It's similar to the SC2 bronze league problem
[2018-03-07 22:43] elyot: there just isn't enough density of players at crap skill levels because they improve too fast
[2018-03-07 22:43] elyot: like you can gain 600 Elo in a few hours
[2018-03-07 22:43] elyot: so there's just a suuuuper low density of people who haven't gained that 600 Elo yet
[2018-03-07 22:43] elyot: like if you don't even know how to absorb properly or buy things in the right order, you get crushed
[2018-03-07 22:44] elyot: and it takes a bit for people to get used to everything to the point where they can have a reasonable game vs other people
[2018-03-07 22:47] elyot: basically people have a much more positive ranked experience if they've already gained that 600 Elo
[2018-03-07 22:47] elyot: if not then they come away with the impression that the game is too hard and everyone is too good and we want to avoid that
[2018-03-07 22:47] elyot: first impression is important
[2018-03-07 22:48] redrame: I guess that is a fair way of looking at it
[2018-03-07 22:48] mqp: ok that was a surprisingly interesting defense of why you would want to gate "high-something" games behind a skill barrier (where "something" is some mix of pressure, anxiety, skill test, frustration)
[2018-03-07 22:49] redrame: difference between something as easy as reading the 307th standard style once and not is prob hundereds of elo
[2018-03-07 22:49] redrame: here's an interesting alternative if that's your justification: why not make expert bot your gatekeeper?
[2018-03-07 22:49] redrame: guarantees the desired skill level for entry

--- Thread (14 messages, 2018-03-10) ---
[2018-03-10 18:51] willmasaur (Will Ma): I think it's undecided, it depends on what the player base looks like in a few weeks
[2018-03-10 18:51] willmasaur (Will Ma): I think most new players already think there's too many units to learn
[2018-03-10 18:52] willmasaur (Will Ma): But obviously seasoned players (myself included) would enjoy the game more if new units got launched
[2018-03-10 18:52] willmasaur (Will Ma): I think one difference from MtG/Hearthstone, where there are 1000 different cards, is that there's much fewer _relevant_ cards
[2018-03-10 18:53] willmasaur (Will Ma): You only have to worry about the cards in your deck, other viable decks, and specifically, the cards your opponent draws in a game
[2018-03-10 18:53] willmasaur (Will Ma): In Prismata all units are relevant and on the "efficient frontier", and also, since it's perfect information, you have to pre-plan for everything in advance
[2018-03-10 18:53] willmasaur (Will Ma): So I would say learning 100 units in Prismata is already more cognitive load than learning 1000 cards in HS/MtG
[2018-03-10 18:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): from a new player point of view - learning 100 units or 110 is similarly overwhealming 😃 (in a good way)
[2018-03-10 18:55] crash_overlord: Considering that point, prismata seems really easy to realease 1 unit at a time while still giving a fresh feeling? As in, you don't need to design a big "set" of 50 new units to release but you can trickle them slowly without losing a lot of satisfaction
[2018-03-10 18:55] crash_overlord: Maybe smaller 5 unit sets would be optimal :clordHmm:
[2018-03-10 18:56] willmasaur (Will Ma): Yeah Crash I think that very much would be a viable option yah
[2018-03-10 18:56] sunfallte (Sunfall): Yeah, I think that dropping new units 3-5 at a time is the right way, with higher chances of them showing up on launch week.
[2018-03-10 18:56] sunfallte (Sunfall): The game has *huge* unit cognitive load, and like @Will Ma said there aren't any equivalents of a 2-drop Green 2/2
[2018-03-10 18:56] sunfallte (Sunfall): so you've got to know them all.
